ALL SPECIALTIES / PHARMACY TECHNICIAN / RHODE ISLAND

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Rhode Island Pharmacy Technician Registration

Rhode Island Pharmacy Technician license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-03-25 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Pharmacy Technician applications in Rhode Island run through Rhode Island Board of Pharmacy. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official Transcripts - Sent directly from your educational institution.
  • Exam Score Report - Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 2 to 4 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Rhode Island by submitting a new application to the Rhode Island Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Rhode Island-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 2 to 4 Weeks.

  1. STEP 1

    1. Register and Submit Online Application

    Create an account on the Rhode Island Department of Health Online Licensing Portal and complete the Pharmacy Technician application form. Provide all requested personal, educational, and professional information.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    2. Fulfill Age and Education Requirements

    Ensure you meet the minimum age requirement of 16 years. You must also have a high school diploma or its equivalent, OR be currently enrolled in a program that awards a high school diploma or its equivalent, OR be enrolled in an accredited pharmacy technician training program.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    3. Complete Criminal Background Check (NCBC)

    Initiate a National Criminal Background Check (NCBC) through the Rhode Island Attorney General's Office or a local police department. Follow the instructions on the RIDOH criminal background checks page (health.ri.gov/forms/criminalbackgroundchecks/) to obtain the necessary BCI form, get fingerprinted, and ensure the results are sent directly to the Rhode Island Department of Health.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    4. Pay the Application Fee

    Submit the non-refundable application fee through the Rhode Island Department of Health Online Licensing Portal as part of your application submission. The current fee for an initial license is $25.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    5. Await Board Review and Licensure

    After submitting your application, all required documentation, and the fee, the Rhode Island Board of Pharmacy will review your submission. Once approved, your Pharmacy Technician license will be issued through the online portal.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$100
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$100

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
Rhode Island Board of Pharmacy
Phone
401-222-2828
Website

Application page
Open application

Renewal

Pharmacy Technician renewal in Rhode Island follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years
Renewal fee
$25
Continuing education
20 hours every two years

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a Rhode Island pharmacy technician license cost?

The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

Is national certification required?

Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

Does Rhode Island have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Rhode Island. To work in Rhode Island, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Rhode Island?

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

Can I start working in Rhode Island while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$100

Processing time

2 to 4 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

See board

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Every 2 years · $25

Licensing board

Rhode Island Board of Pharmacy
